chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-05 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 13.01.2026 Mian Taj Muhammad Keerio advocate a/w petitioner *** In continuation of the Court's query recorded in the Order dated 01.01.2026 regarding the maintainability of this petition, learned counsel for the petitioner has drawn attention to the representation dated 30.09.2025 (available at pages 15–19 of the Court file). He submits that the document referred to in the earlier Order was merely a reminder, whereas the original representation seeking extension of the petitioner's deputation period remains pending before the Chairperson, BISP. Learned counsel further submits that despite the pendency of the said representation, the Zonal Director, South Zone, BISP Hyderabad, through Order dated 01.01.2026, has relieved the petitioner from service. It is argued that the Zonal Director lacks competence to decide or act upon the matter of deputation extension. Counsel states that he would be satisfied if the petition is disposed of with a direction to the Chairperson BISP to decide the petitioner's representation, and that, in the meantime, the relieving Order may not be given effect. Mr. Bashir Ahmed Almani, learned Assistant Attorney General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and raises no objection to the proposal advanced by learned counsel for the petitioner. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with the direction that the Chairperson, BISP, shall decide the petitioner's representation, referred to hereinabove, within sixty days from the date of this Order, strictly in accordance with law.

Bashir Ahmed Almani, learned Assistant Attorney General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and raises no objection to the proposal advanced by learned counsel for the petitioner. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with the direction that the Chairperson, BISP, shall decide the petitioner's representation, referred to hereinabove, within sixty days from the date of this Order, strictly in accordance with law. Until such a decision is made, the relieving Order dated 01.01.2026 shall remain in abeyance and shall not be acted upon. Let a copy of this Order be transmitted to the Chairperson, BISP, for information and compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
